flares.note: Occulted flare on the west limb from the point of view of GOES.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the NE in GOES CCOR-1, SOHO LASCO C2/C3, and to the NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source for this CME is seen as opening field lines around 2026-07-04T23:05Z in GOES SUVI 284 imagery beyond the limb. There is outflowing material before and after this front.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Possible streamer blowout CME to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and to the N/NW in STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is no clear source for this CME, however there are continued moving/opening field lines visible along the northern limb in GOES SUVI 284 imagery starting around 2026-07-05T00:00Z. There are numerous fronts and outflows ahead and behind of this CME, however the features behind this CME are included in this fit.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the W/NW in SOHO LASCO C2. It is not clearly seen in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, or STEREO A COR2. The source is a C9.9 flare from AR 14479 with associated filament ejecta visible to the W in GOES SUVI 304 starting around 2026-07-05T07:13Z. The source is also visible in SDO/AIA and STEREO A EUVI wavelengths.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on source location and best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 frames.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T11:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-07-05T10:09Z to 2026-07-05T17:23Z. There is no clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to likely far sided source and lack of multi-viewpoint imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T16:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-07-05T10:09Z to 2026-07-05T17:23Z. There is no clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to likely source beyond the East limb and lack of COR2A imagery to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW beginning at 2026-07-05T17:00Z in SOHO LASCO C2, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2. The source of this event is an M1.4 flare from AR 14479 (N16W88) peaking at 2026-07-05T16:32Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. A wide EUV wave and field line opening extending to the East of the flare (out to approx. East 50) is also visible in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the East in SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T17:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. The source of this event is a small eruption seen located at approx. S09E18 beginning at approx. 2026-07-05T16:15Z as seen in SDO AIA 131 and 193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the West by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-05T22:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is an M1.0 flare from AR 14479 (N16W91) which peaked at 2026-07-05T21:22Z as seen in SDO AIA 131.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is  filament eruption seen lifting off starting around 2026-07-06T05:00Z in SDO AIA 304 imagery. The eruption is also best seen as dimming and moving field lines in SDO AIA 193. The filament is centered around N15E60.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as the source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME partially overlaps in the field of view with CME with ID: 2026-07-06T06:24:00-CME-001. The potential source is not clear in EUV imagery but may be a small eruption from around N05E20 seen around 2026-07-06T04:15Z with brightening in GOES SUVI 304 imagery. This eruption is near a coronal hole as seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery and may exhibit deflection to the east.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely a C6.1 flare and eruption seen from AR 14479 in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery around N16W90, with the flare peaking at 2026-07-06T05:11Z. The eruption is also visible off the NW limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery in SWPC_CAT. The leading edge rapidly becomes difficult to measure in imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Potentially faintly visible to the west in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely an eruption from Active Region 14479 (N15W95) as seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery and on the west limb in GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-07-06T09:17Z. This is potentially associated with a C-class flare as seen in GOES data, but the source attribution in available EUV imagery is unclear.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, not observed in STEREO A COR2 likely due to the occulting disk or CME being too faint from its point-of-view. The source is likely an eruption from former AR 14480/14477, observed in SDO AIA 171/193/304 as filament ejecta and field line movement over the SW limb starting around 2026-07-06T17:06Z, and as dimming in the SW vicinity of former AR 14480/14477 in STEREO A EUVI 195 starting at 2026-07-06T17:35, around approximately S10-S20 and W110-W120.

cmeAnalyses.note: SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only 3D analysis using the approximate observed source location of W110-W120 to fit at W115. The longitude and other parameters could vary slightly as a result.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. No distinct source on the disk is observed, but loop brightening on/beyond the west limb in SDO AIA 171 starting around 2026-07-06T17:18Z suggests former AR 14479 over the west limb is a likely weak eruptive source.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W only in SOHO LASCO C2. Source is likely former AR 14479, with brightening and filament ejecta in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 at 2026-07-06T18:25Z centered approximately ~N10W110. Also observed in SDO AIA 171/193/304 as filament ejecta and field line movement around the west limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2 only fit as the only observing coronagraph, using the approximate source ~N10W110 to fit longitude, as such longitude and other parameters could vary slightly.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE only in STEREO A COR2, not observed in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely AR 14483 with field line movement and brightening centered ~S07W40, followed by dimming, starting around 2026-07-06T18:58Z in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D STEREO A COR2 only analysis using AR 14483 as the source longitude, and parameters could vary as a result. CME half width is likely less than 10 degrees. No visibility in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 or GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Narrow, faint CME visible to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2/C3. Not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 or STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source is an eruption with dimming around N20E40 starting at 2026-07-07T03:52Z best seen in SDO AIA 193 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source location. The leading edge is unclear, and visible in limited running difference imagery, so this measurement is more speculative than usual.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The potential source may be an eruption from AR 14479 (approximately N15W105) as seen in STEREO A EUV 195/304 imagery starting around 2026-07-07T05:45Z. The eruption is best seen as brightening in both wavelengths.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on source location using SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-07T14:48Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 1016-07-07T14:23Z to 2026-07-07T18:23Z. There is no clear source seen for this event as it is likely far sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using longitude of E90 due to source beyond the NE limb and lack of STEREO A COR2 imagery to triangulate source location.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-07T15:12Z. This CME fades before exiting the field of view in SOHO LASCO C2 and is therefore not visible in other coronagraphs. The source of this event is an M4.0 flare from Active Region 14482 (S11E50) peaking at 2026-07-07T14:19Z as seen in SDO AIA 131. Dimming and field line movement is also visible in SDO AIA 171/193, along with some filament material seen in SDO AIA 304, although most of this material appears to get reabsorbed and falls back to the solar surface.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NE beginning at 2026-07-07T22:12Z by SOHO LASCO C2. The source of this event is a large filament eruption seen centered at approx. N20E70 with liftoff beginning at approx. 2026-07-07T21:33Z as seen in SDO AIA and GOES SUVI 304. Field line opening, dimming and post eruptive arcades are also seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME visible to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. Potentially faintly seen to the west in STEREO A COR2. The source is beyond the west limb as seen as opening field lines in GOES SUVI 284 imagery starting at 2026-07-07T23:05Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and the potential corresponding faint CME leading edge in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SW in SOHO GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This CME was not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time due to a data gap from 2026-07-08T05:48Z to 2026-07-08T15:48Z. The potential source is an eruption best seen as brightening moving field lines in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery from AR 4478 (approximately S15W110) as seen from the point of view of STEREO A starting at 2026-07-08T05:55Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the faint shock based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 data in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SW in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME was not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time due to a data gap from 2026-07-08T05:48Z to 2026-07-08T15:48Z. This event overlaps in the field of view with CME: 2026-07-08T06:45Z. The potential source may be an eruption from AR 14478 (approx. S05W113) or 14479 (approx. N17W121) as seen in STEREO A EUVI imagery, with moving field lines and brightening seen from the regions around 2026-07-08T06:00Z-07:00Z, but it is difficult to tell from the perspective of STEREO A EUVI imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as the potential source location.

cmes.note: CME faintly visible to the north in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME is not clearly visible in GOES CCOR-1 imagery and not seen in SOHO LASCO imagery in real-time due to a downlink data gap. There is no source found, and the CME may be farsided with respect to STEREO A as well as SDO imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane of sky measurement using STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the west in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME was not visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time due to a data gap from 2026-07-08T05:48Z to 2026-07-08T15:48Z. The source is likely an eruption with opening field lines starting around 2026-07-08T08:25Z in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery. The exact source location on the disk as seen from STEREO A is unclear, as the source is near the west limb.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on potential source location as well as the best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME visible to the W in STEREO A COR2 imagery. This CME is also visible via difference imagery in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery to the NW. There is no clear source for this CME.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Possibly two overlapping CMEs visible to the E in STEREO A COR2 imagery and to the S/SW in GOES CCOR-1 imagery starting at 2026-07-09T07:53Z and 2026-07-09T08:23Z based on STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source of this CME(s) is an eruption from the area of Active Region 14485 (S10W34) starting around 2026-07-09T06:56Z to the SW in GOES SUVI 195 with additional eruption signatures to the NW of AR 14485 (S10W34) in GOES SUVI 195 starting around 2026-07-09T07:08Z. There is a following eruption starting around 2026-07-09T07:48Z with associated brightening and dimming to the N/NW of the Active Region spanning roughly from W25 to W10 and S00 to S12. An EUV wave is visible in GOES SUVI 284 following the main eruption with post-eruptive arcades starting around 2026-07-09T08:53Z as seen in GOES SUVI 195 and 284. These eruptions are also visible in STEREO A EUV 195 and 304 as well as in SDO/AIA wavelengths following an eclipse.

cmeAnalyses.note: Parameters based on best fit using STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery including both fronts or lobes of the CME(s) visible more clearly in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the east in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -27 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap from 2026-07-09T14:53Z to 2026-07-09T23:53Z. The source may be related to an eruption from Active Region 14484 (N15W50) starting around 2026-07-09T20:45Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/193/304 and GOES SUVI 131/195/284/304 imagery. A subsequent opening of field lines is visible over the northwest limb at this time in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 284 imagery as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the source longitude. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery preventing a triangulated measurement, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is far-sided from Earth, observed as field line movement beyond the West limb in GOES SUVI 171/195/284 starting roughly around 2026-07-10T09:21Z. May also be observed in STEREO A EUVI 195 on its west limb over former AR 14479, with loops over this region brightening starting around 2026-07-10T08:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the SE in SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. The source is a minor eruption centered around S20E60, observed as dimming and field line movement in GOES SUVI 195 starting around 2026-07-10T07:28Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CMe observed to the E in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This front is overtaken by CME: 2026-07-10T15:53Z in STEREO A COR2's field-of-view only. The source is a filament eruption near the east limb centered ~N06E63 beginning as early as 2026-07-10T12:18Z in SDO AIA 304 with the filament brightening and agitating, and more eruptively lifting off around 2026-07-10T13:33Z although the majority of the material seems to reabsorb onto the disk. Also observed as field line movement in SDO AIA 171/193.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the SW in STEREO A COR2, GOES CCOR-1, and SOHO LASCO C2/C3 after a downlink gap backfilled in. The source is AR 14485 and a C6.0 flare (S16W47) peaking at 2026-07-10T14:59Z. Also observed well in SDO AIA 171/193 as field line movement, pockets of dimming in the vicinity (not a central area), and a minor EUV wave.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, using the width in STEREO A COR2 as it appears much less wide in SOHO's field-of-view. Significant overlap with CME: 2026-07-10T13:26Z in COR2's field-of-view muddles a good view of the leading edge for most frames.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 of a faint shock feature best observed in LASCO C2's field-of-view, noting it does measure slower than the bulk.

cmes.note: CME observed to the NW only in SOHO LASCO C2, likely due to diffusing and becoming too faint ot resolve after earliest frames. The source is likely a weak filament ejecta and field line movement centered ~N15W65 in SDO AIA 171/193 starting around 2026-07-10T23:15Z, in proximity to a nearby coronal hole.

cmeAnalyses.note: 3D SOHO LASCO C2 only analysis using the observed source around W65 as an estimate, as such parameters could slightly vary.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NE in STEREO A COR2, and NNW in SOHO LASCO C2, also NNW but heavily pylon obscured in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely a large, diffuse area of slow dimming and field line movement on the NW disk in SDO AIA 171/193, starting as early as 2026-07-11T20:30Z and centered approximately around N45W35. This diffuse, slow source is likely also responsible for the following, overlapping CME: 2026-07-11T04:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, as the CME is blocked by the pylon in LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and also too faint to resolve in running difference imagery beyond earliest frames.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the NE in STEREO A COR2, and NNW in SOHO LASCO C2, also NNW but heavily pylon obscured in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source is likely a large, diffuse area of slow dimming and field line movement on the NW disk in SDO AIA 171/193, starting as early as 2026-07-11T20:30Z and centered approximately around N45W35, and may be separable with more weak field line movement to the NW in SDO AIA 171 around 2026-07-11T02:51Z but it is not too distinct. This diffuse, slow source is likely also responsible for the preceeding, overlapping CME: 2026-07-11T04:38Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, as the CME is partially blocked by the pylon in LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2 and W/SW GOES CCOR-1, not in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time due to an ongoing outage. The source of the CME is likely former Active Region 14479 or AR 14478, estimated to be located ~N17W165 and ~S05W155 at this time. There are two potential eruptive signatures observed on/around the west limb in STEREO A EUVI: one as wide field line breaking and EUV wave in EUVI 195 to the SW starting around 2026-07-11T15:05Z, and prominence movement and brightening in EUVI 304 to the NW starting around 2026-07-11T15:45Z. Of these, the SW field line movement appears to be a better fit based on timing and CME LE/SH appearance.

cmeAnalyses.note: Bulk leading edge triangulation between STEREO A COR and GOES CCOR-1.
Shock front triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in STEREO A COR2 and W/SW GOES CCOR-1 following behind the 2026-07-11T16:09Z CME, not in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 in real-time due to an ongoing outage. The source of the CME may likely involve former Active Region 14479 or AR 14478, estimated to be located ~N17W165 and ~S05W155 at this time.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary, very speculative leading edge measurement just relying on two frames in STEREO A COR2A and assumption of the source based on the appearance with respect to the 16:09Z CME preceding this eruption.

cmes.note: CME seen to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2 in association with a long-duration C5.3-class flare from AR 4485 (S15W70) with corresponding circular EUV wave seen best in GOES SUVI 284 imagery starting around 2026-07-12T01:00Z. The CME is seen in just two C2 frames so far and is still in progress.

cmeAnalyses.note: Preliminary leading edge measurement using only the first two frames of SOHO C2 and assuming a modest 15 degrees of deflection eastward relative to the source longitude due to the presence of a coronal hole feature.
